Summary.valueString: The video begins with a Flight Simulator plane flying over an island, introducing a collaboration with Microsoft Azure AI. A person in a modern office setting discusses the importance of good data for neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, mentioning a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances for natural voice generation. Visuals show audio waveforms representing the data used. The scene shifts to aerial views of a data center facility, emphasizing the scale and infrastructure supporting the technology. Another person explains the high fidelity and human-like quality of cognitive services' TTS offerings. The video then transitions to detailed Flight Simulator scenes showing planes taxiing and preparing for pushback, with realistic airport ground crew and aircraft visuals. The segment ends with the Microsoft logo, reinforcing the partnership and technology integration.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 2
transcriptPhrases.text: To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.
